verdict.answer_first: B states the core problem in its first sentence, "The main problem is the unguarded property access", whereas A's first sentence is introductory throat-clearing.
verdict.decisiveness: B commits to a single fix, presenting it as "A safer version:", while A presents two different code blocks as co-equal options, forcing me to make the design choice.
verdict.fabrication: A invents context not provided in the question: "Want me to look at how this is actually called in the codebase..."
verdict.actionability: B is more actionable because it's shorter. It gives the most common fix and a brief note on the alternative, letting me fix the bug in seconds without reading extra hardening advice.
